It's a myth that doesn't work with affluence. There's a $27 million house for sale right now in Reno. And the premium with purchase is you get the guys Bentley and his Rolls Royce that are in the garage. So it's right there. I mean they're not making any secret of it. So all of those things go into the offer. Then the structure of the offer is what order do you present? Do you lead with the risk reversal? Do you lead with the price to value differential? Do you lead with the premium? Do you, what order do you put these things in? What emphasis do you put on them? And how do you figure that out? Well, you can, you know, put them on a wall and have a monkey throw darts. I mean you can. Right? That's the GKIC method. If you are. Don't tell all our secrets. If you are. Hey, at least we're using darts. If you. We leased the monkey though. It comes in once a quarter. And he's skinny because there's no budget for bananas. Oh boy. Oh boy. So you can do that, right? If you are very aware of your customer, how they think, how they operate, etc. And you're fairly skilled at marketing, you can figure out what is the likely best. If you can, you will get to it by testing. So often you need to find a way to test that then migrates down to the salesperson. So if right now if you go into sleep number bed store, the way the offer is presented to you, which leads with risk reversal, and they all do it one way. They got there by split testing in their direct response advertising and marketing and in their phone room. And then they got to a best script, which then gets locked in at the sales level. Well that's the beauty of the folks who are watching this who are GKIC members because they know how to do this, right? With sales letters and because really it's salesmanship and print. That's what a sales letter is. And so it's going to be basically the same structure. If you get it to work in the sales letter, I mean for most cases you can follow that same structure with one on one salesperson as well. In many cases if you could get away with it, you'd be better off having the salesperson sit there and read them the sales letter than you would doing anything else. So yeah, it migrates. So here's the thing that we've heard many times, Nick, and I want you to address this because it's a problem. This offer is so good that I don't really have to sell it. People will just know that they want it. Well, it could be further from the truth. But I mean, well, we've heard it. We think we hear it, right? And I think we think that as business owners or as the person crafting the offer. But really when you're talking about face to face selling, belly button to belly button or if you're doing it over the phone in the phone room, there needs to be some level of presenting the offer, walking them through it. You're also testing, I think, Dan, you're absolutely right of. You're listening to what gets people excited, what gets people, you know, it's really not a benefit for me. But the thing that they offer so good, we don't have to tell anybody about it. They're just going to find their way to us. I think it's the quickest way to bankrupt people. Well, not even find their way to us, but I think the offer is so good that I, or let's take it to the extreme. It's a free offer. I'm offering a free consultation. I'm offering a free book. I'm offering, I don't have to sell it. Dan, how would you address that? So, so the departed Joan Rivers said, even the ugliest girl on the planet with an opportunity to sleep with Brad Pitt would still appreciate being asked. So, but people appreciate being asked to buy. Right? So first of all, to his point, it usually has to be explained for it to be understood as such a great offer. Secondly, people like to be asked to buy. They don't really like arrogant selling. They don't like their purchase being taken for granted.
